title: Postmortem - Incident #4217: Database Connection Pool Exhaustion
content:
Postmortem - Incident #4217: Database Connection Pool Exhaustion

### Incident Summary
On March 12, the api-gateway-prod service went offline for 45 minutes due to connection pool exhaustion. Incoming requests received 504 gateway timeout errors.

### Root Cause
Deployment of api-gateway-prod v2.2 introduced a database transaction block that did not properly release the connection back to the pool. When incoming request rates peaked, all 5 available pool connections were held indefinitely in an 'idle in transaction' state.

### Resolution
The on-call engineer identified the idle transactions and terminated them using pg_terminate_backend. The connection pool size was increased from 5 to 15 in the configuration to provide immediate relief. A code rollback to v2.1 was executed to resolve the transaction release bug.

### Action Items
- Add a middleware rule to enforce connection release on request completion.
- Set transaction timeouts to automatic timeout of 60 seconds.
